_comment: Add a _ before an option name to comment it out and it wont be added to the apworld

goal._comment: Using 'goal' as the name of an option let you override some of the generated goal parameters
goal.description: Set which boss must be defeated to complete the randomizer. If an unreachable boss is chosen, generation will fail.

filler_traps._comment: Using values here let you set a 'recommended' filler_trap percentage or even a default percentage

description: Select your wizard's primary school. Choose 'random-school' to select a school at random with equal odds.

Select your wizard's secondary school. If you choose the same school as the primary, a different school will be chosen at random. Choose 'any' to allow yourself to pick a secondary school during the game. This will cause secondary spell items to appear as e.g. 'Secondary L1'.
Ensures the Teleport-Mark ability is given at the start or prior to various checkpoints. Mid-streets refers to the Cyclops/Fodder/Bubbles, Harvest Lord/Nexus, and Fodder/Bastilla quests.

Ensures the Slot-Mount ability is given at the start or prior to various checkpoints. Mid-streets refers to the Cyclops/Fodder/Bubbles, Harvest Lord/Nexus, and Fodder/Bastilla quests. Placing this at Mid-Streets or Anywhere is extremely not recommended.
Guarantee at least one rank 2 spell prior to various checkpoints. Mid-streets refers to the Cyclops/Fodder/Bubbles, Harvest Lord/Nexus, and Fodder/Bastilla quests.
Guarantee at least one World/Commons/Home teleport buttons prior to various checkpoints. Mid-streets refers to the Cyclops/Fodder/Bubbles, Harvest Lord/Nexus, and Fodder/Bastilla quests.
Enables Friendly Teleports in logic and grants access to them in the starting inventory. Enabling this creates a more varied earlygame (pre-Rattlebones).
Enable this setting to disable unintuitive logic and improve the starting inventory.

Adds locations for collecting reagents in Wizard City. Select 'anywhere' to add one location per reagent type (6 locations). Select 'anywhere-ore' to only add the Ore location, which has the most interesting access logic. Select 'all' to add reagent locations per area: Unicorn Way, Commons, Ravenwood, Shopping District, Olde Town, Cyclops Lane, Triton Avenue, Haunted Cave, Firecat Alley, and Dark Cave. (Adds up to 26 locations)
Adds a location for each Halloween-exclusive quest, adding about 20 locations and 13 items.
Adds a location for each Housing Item you can pick up in Wizard City, adding up to 5 locations.

Adds locations for wooden chests in Wizard City. Select 'anywhere' to add one wooden chest location, or 'all' to add wooden chest locations for Unicorn Way, Triton Avenue, Firecat Alley, Cyclops Lane, Haunted Cave, and Dark Cave.
Adds locations for silver chests in Wizard City. Select 'anywhere' to add one silver chest location, or 'all' to add silver chest locations for Triton Avenue, Firecat Alley, and Cyclops Lane.
Adds locations for finding the Smiths for Zeke. Select 'quest' for a single combined location, or 'all' to add individual locations for each area. (Adds up to 9 locations)
Half module includes Golem Court, full module includes Golem Court and Golem Tower. Default full, adds about 1-2 items and 4-8 locations.
Include all quests that visit the Pet Pavilion. Default on, adds about 1 item and 4 locations. This is currently broken in live so is disabled until fixed.
Half module includes everything up to Eyus, full module includes everything in Cyclops. Default full, adds about 2-5 items and 8-15 locations.
Half module includes Kraken and Skullsplitter, full module includes everything in Triton. Default full, adds about 3-6 items and 8-19 locations.
Half module includes Larry and Wormguts, full module includes everything in Firecat. Default full, adds about 3-7 items and 9-14 locations.
Includes everything made available after either one or all three streets are completed, adding about 1-3 items and 12-14 locations.
This is a Toggle
A simple boolean, aka True or False set the initial value with "default"
This is a Choice
Let the user pick from a list of values
allow_custom_value let a player define their own text value
Currently the only use for Choice in Manual outside of hooks is the goal
This is a Range
Allow the player to specify a value between 'start' and 'end'
if you include a "values" you can define the label for certain values
Currently the only use for Range in Manual outside of hooks is 'filler_trap'
